title: Ranger 5 (Atlas-Agena B)
description: Open datasets and source materials relevant to Ranger 5 (1962), the sixth consecutive Ranger failure — solar panel power loss, 725 km lunar miss, heliocentric orbit — Hylocomium splendens (stair-step moss), Northern Rough-winged Swallow (Stelgidopteryx serripennis), and spacecraft power system reliability

sources.name: NSSDC Master Catalog — Ranger 5
sources.description: NASA Space Science Data Coordinated Archive entry for Ranger 5. NSSDC designation 1962-055A. Spacecraft description (342.5 kg, Block II design), instrument manifest (TV camera, gamma-ray spectrometer, radar altimeter, seismometer capsule), Atlas-Agena B launch vehicle, power failure timeline (solar panel short circuit at T+75 minutes, battery depletion at T+8h44m), 725 km lunar miss distance, and heliocentric orbit insertion. Documents the gamma-ray data collected during the 4-hour powered flight.
sources.mission_relevance: Definitive source for Ranger 5 spacecraft specifications, failure timeline, and mission outcome. Documents the power-switching malfunction, battery life, and final tracking to 1.27 million km using the capsule's 50 mW transmitter.

sources.name: NASA Science — Ranger 5 Mission Page
sources.description: NASA's official Ranger 5 mission page summarizing the Block II spacecraft, launch on October 18, 1962, power malfunction at T+15 minutes (NASA's timing differs from other sources — likely referring to the first anomaly indication), battery depletion, and 725 km lunar flyby.
sources.mission_relevance: Official NASA mission summary providing context for the congressional investigation and program reorganization that followed six consecutive Ranger failures.

sources.name: JPL Technical Report 32-199 — Scientific Experiments for Rangers 3, 4, and 5
sources.description: Caltech/JPL technical report documenting the scientific experiments carried on Block II Rangers. Describes the gamma-ray spectrometer, TV camera, radar altimeter, and seismometer capsule. Includes instrument specifications, data formats, and the science objectives that were never achieved.
sources.mission_relevance: Provides the instrument specifications needed to calculate what Ranger 5 could have measured at 725 km flyby distance, and documents the 4 hours of gamma-ray background data that were collected before power loss.

sources.name: JPL HORIZONS Ephemeris System — Ranger 5
sources.description: JPL ephemeris computation service providing heliocentric orbital elements for Ranger 5 as an artificial solar system body. Perihelion ~0.95 AU, aphelion ~1.05 AU, period ~365 days. Enables current position computation.
sources.mission_relevance: Essential for the heliocentric ghost knowledge node — computing Ranger 5's current position relative to Earth and confirming its near-Earth-like solar orbit.

sources.name: Siddiqi, A.A. — Beyond Earth: A Chronicle of Deep Space Exploration, 1958-2016
sources.description: NASA History Program Office publication providing comprehensive chronological coverage of all deep space missions including Ranger 5. Documents the failure sequence, congressional investigation, and JPL reorganization that followed.
sources.mission_relevance: Authoritative historical context for the six consecutive Ranger failures and the institutional crisis they provoked.

sources.name: USDA Forest Service — Hylocomium splendens Ecology
sources.description: USDA Fire Effects Information System entry for Hylocomium splendens (stair-step moss). Covers distribution, habitat requirements, growth rate, desiccation tolerance, fire response, and ecological role in PNW old-growth forests. Annual growth increments of 2-3 cm form the characteristic stair-step morphology.
sources.mission_relevance: Establishes the biological and ecological context for the organism pairing. The moss's desiccation tolerance — surviving complete water loss and resuming metabolism upon rehydration — is the central resonance with Ranger 5's irreversible power loss.

sources.name: Oregon State University — Andrews Forest Bryophyte Data
sources.description: Long-term ecological research data from the H.J. Andrews Experimental Forest including bryophyte community surveys, biomass measurements, and desiccation/rehydration studies on forest floor mosses including Hylocomium splendens.
sources.mission_relevance: Provides quantitative data for the desiccation tolerance knowledge node: hydration thresholds, recovery times, and annual growth rates of stair-step moss in PNW old-growth forests.

sources.name: Cornell Lab of Ornithology — Northern Rough-winged Swallow
sources.description: Species account for Stelgidopteryx serripennis covering identification, habitat, behavior, nesting ecology, and the mysterious serrated wing feathers. Documents the solitary nesting behavior that distinguishes this species from colonial swallows.
sources.mission_relevance: Establishes the behavioral context for the bird pairing: the solitary, drab, unremarkable swallow as a counterpart to Ranger 5's quiet, unremarked failure.

sources.name: NASA Image and Video Library — Ranger Program
sources.description: Historical photographs from the Ranger program: Block II spacecraft assembly at JPL, Atlas-Agena B on the launch pad at Cape Canaveral LC-12, solar panel deployment tests, and the post-failure investigation. Public domain imagery.
sources.mission_relevance: Visual documentation of the Ranger 5 spacecraft, the identical Block II design shared with Rangers 3 and 4, and the solar panel configuration whose failure ended the mission.

sources.name: Jonathan McDowell's Launch Vehicle Database
sources.description: Comprehensive launch database documenting the Atlas-Agena B vehicle performance for Ranger 5, confirming the launch vehicle performed nominally. The failure was entirely in the spacecraft.
sources.mission_relevance: Contextualizes Ranger 5 within the Ranger program's launch record and the broader Atlas-Agena performance history. Confirms that the Block II spacecraft, not the launch vehicle, was the locus of failure.
